root_motivation: Agent behavior is invisible between releases — the planner, binders, and pillar tools can silently change what they do to a given question, and no automated gate will catch it. Replaying logged decisions through the current agent and diffing the output turns each past decision into a regression test, making behavioral drift a first-class CI signal rather than a silent surprise.
user_persona: MQO platform engineer who ships changes to mqo-agent or its pillar tools and needs to verify no previously-observed behavior regressed before merging.

acceptance_criteria.description: `run` extracts every question from a fixture log and produces one fresh record per question via the (mocked) agent subprocess.
`diff` against a no-change fixture agent classifies every item `unchanged`.
`diff` against a drifted fixture agent correctly labels at least one each of `plan_drift`, `bind_drift`, and `outcome_drift`, naming the question.
`value_drift` fires only when the numeric answer moves beyond `--value-tol` and not within it (boundary tested at the tolerance edge).
`report --fail-on bind_drift,value_drift` exits non-zero when those classes are present and zero when only `plan_drift` is present.
Numeric comparison matches `mqo-engine-parity`'s tolerance convention: absolute tolerance by default (1e-6), relative optional; same inputs yield same verdict.
Determinism: `diff`/`report` output is stable across runs for fixed inputs; no wall-clock leaks into the classification.
`serve` answers the replay tool calls; `--help` documents every flag; all tests run cluster-free against bundled fixtures.

scope: Re-run NL questions from a mqo-decision-log through the current mqo-agent (or a mock) as a subprocess
Classify per-question deltas into: unchanged, plan_drift, bind_drift, outcome_drift, value_drift
Render a summary report and exit non-zero on specified drift classes
Serve run/diff/report as MCP tools via `serve` subcommand
Run entirely offline against bundled fixture logs and fixture agents
non_goals: Not a golden-accuracy benchmark (that is mqo-bench)
Does not re-run against live warehouses in tests
Does not auto-fix drift — reports and gates only
Does not compare against model contract (that is mqo-semantic-regression)

additional.subprocess_interface: mqo-agent or any cmd overridable via --agent flag

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a replay tool instead of relying on existing regression tests?
five_whys_trace.a: Existing tests (mqo-semantic-regression) watch the model contract — grain, PII tags. They do not watch whether the agent's planner calls the same pillars or reaches the same outcome for the same question after a code change.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does agent behavioral drift matter if the model contract is unchanged?
five_whys_trace.a: A planner change could route the same question through different pillars (bind_drift), switch from 'answered' to 'clarify' (outcome_drift), or return a numerically different value (value_drift) without touching any measure's grain or PII tag.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not just replay in a unit test within mqo-agent itself?
five_whys_trace.a: mqo-decision-log is the corpus: it captures production decisions across all sessions. A unit test inside mqo-agent only covers what the author anticipated; the log captures the actual question distribution users posed.

five_whys_trace.q: Why make it a standalone CLI rather than a script?
five_whys_trace.a: CI needs a stable interface: a binary with a structured diff output and exit codes that block a release on bind_drift without requiring CI to parse unstructured logs.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does this need to be its own repo rather than a module inside mqo-agent?
five_whys_trace.a: It must run against any version of mqo-agent as a subprocess — coupling it inside the agent would create a circular dependency and prevent it from being the independent observer it needs to be.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What does 'bind_drift' mean exactly — is it the bound measure name or also grain?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: bind_drift captures any change to what was bound: measure name, grain, or numeric value field in the decision record. value_drift is the numeric-only sub-case with tolerance.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How does the agent subprocess get invoked?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: mqo-replay run passes the question as stdin (or --question arg) to the agent subprocess and expects a JSON record on stdout matching DecisionRecord schema. --agent <cmd> overrides the default 'mqo-agent'.
